scoreChanges.name: Anthropic
scoreChanges.headline: METHODOLOGY NOTE: Delta is primarily a calculation artifact — published 90.9 included an integration premium (~+27) exceeding the v1.1 H1 cap of +10. Event-driven delta is approximately −2 (INT 3.4→3.0 on Ballard Partners lobbying firm hire, April 13). Canonical formula score ~63.4 is consistent with prior evidence-based assessment. SB 3261 support (child-safety AI liability) is a positive INT/ACC signal active pre-April 24 Illinois committee vote.

scoreChanges.name: Amazon
scoreChanges.headline: Band crossing Developing → Critical. PDX9 Troutdale worker death (April 6): management ordered employees to work around body for 1+ hour; CPR-trained worker told not to help. NLRB April 2 bargaining order at JFK8 — first-ever union bargaining order against Amazon. Facility has 6x national warehouse injury rate average. High confidence.

scoreChanges.name: IBM
scoreChanges.headline: First evidence-based assessment. $17M DOJ FCA settlement (April 10) — first-ever resolution under Civil Rights Fraud Initiative. Allegations: race/sex-based workforce goals tied to bonus compensation; discriminatory program access over 7 years (2019–2026). Published 3.5×8 uniform profile confirmed as Gen-2 methodology artifact. Partial cooperation credit applied.

scoreChanges.name: Palantir AI
scoreChanges.headline: First evidence-based baseline. EFF April 2026: Palantir did not respond to accountability questions on ImmigrationOS human-rights due diligence. $30M ICE contract for real-time immigrant tracking with documented high-confidence errors. 30 lawmakers demand congressional oversight answers. Former employees open letter: company abandoned founding values. I2 anchor 1 pattern — human-rights policy applied only where reputationally convenient.

scoreChanges.name: Democratic Republic of Congo
scoreChanges.headline: April 18 Montreux ceasefire mechanism (Qatar-mediated) is a partial BND positive — 477 detainee releases, civilian protection commitment. Immediately violated: drone strikes hit South Kivu same day, 4 civilians killed. M23 demands blanket war-crimes immunity — blocks ACC anchor progression. Published score pre-dates 2025–26 M23 escalation including fall of Goma (January 2025).

scoreChanges.name: Deere & Company
scoreChanges.headline: First evidence-based assessment. 598 Iowa + 238 Midwest layoffs since 2025; 4,500+ cumulative since 2015. Mexico expansion concurrent with US job cuts creates I2/I4 inconsistency. Partial positives: executive pay freeze including salaried employees; $20B US manufacturing commitment; gradual worker recall beginning 2026. Gen-2 uniform-score artifact at rank 87 confirmed.

scoreChanges.name: Macy's
scoreChanges.headline: First evidence-based assessment. 1,050 CT warehouse workers permanently laid off by April 30; 150-store closure trajectory by 2028 under 'Bold New Chapter' restructuring. No union representation, no bumping rights. Closures driving 274% industry-wide retail layoff surge. Gen-2 uniform 3.0×8 artifact at rank 88 confirmed. Proposed 41.3 at Functional/Developing boundary.

scoreChanges.name: Interpublic Group
scoreChanges.headline: ENTITY NOTE: IPG ceased as standalone entity November 26, 2025 (Omnicom acquisition). Index entry requires founder decision: remove, merge, or annotate. Score change reflects: 800 pre-close layoffs; $1.5B synergy target doubled post-close; 4,000+ total headcount reductions projected. Gen-2 uniform 3.0×8 artifact at rank 89 confirmed. Band change Functional → Developing.

scoreChanges.name: Norway
scoreChanges.headline: First evidence-based baseline. Published 100 is a methodology ceiling artifact — perfect anchor-5 across all 40 subdimensions is empirically unreachable. Evidence supports decisive Exemplary classification at 84.7. Gaps: Norwegian Oil Fund ~4.5% in fossil fuels; government refused domestic phase-out target; Sámi Fosen wind-farm litigation unresolved. Recommend setting empirical ceiling at 95 across all indexes.

scoreChanges.name: New Zealand
scoreChanges.headline: First evidence-based baseline. Band change Exemplary → Established. Te Tiriti constitutional framework exceptional (Treaty Bill defeated 112-11, April 2025). Māori life-expectancy gap ~7 years persists as EQ2/EQ3 anchor failure. Coalition disestablished Māori Health Authority — I1 policy reversal pressure. Abuse in Care Royal Commission redress incomplete. Established band is well-supported.

confirmations.name: OpenAI
confirmations.note: PRE-VOTE: SB 3444 advocacy (mass-casualty liability shield for AI) unchanged. April 27 Musk trial jury selection begins Oakland. Published 31.3 within tolerance — LinkedIn post copy does not require revision.

confirmations.name: Meta AI
confirmations.note: Keylogger 'Model Capability Initiative' (April 21–22 announcement, employee protests) compounds prior verdict cluster. EU/UK GDPR enforcement inquiry possible — would trigger upgrade to T1 and proposal. Directional within threshold.

confirmations.name: xAI/Grok
confirmations.note: Musk no-show April 20 Paris prosecutor summons. French CSAM/deepfakes/Holocaust denial investigation escalating; prosecutors allege possible deliberate orchestration. Floor held at 2.2.

confirmations.name: Scale AI
confirmations.note: First-time baseline. DOL investigation dropped May 2025 but civil lawsuits remain active. Wage-theft/misclassification anchors EMP/BND. Developing band confirmed.

confirmations.name: Waymo
confirmations.note: First-time baseline. Dual NHTSA/NTSB investigations for child-strike and school-bus-passing incidents. Voluntary Dec 2025 software recall (3,067 vehicles) is AB2 positive. Functional band confirmed.

confirmations.name: UnitedHealth Group
confirmations.note: Q1 beat ($7.23 EPS vs $6.57 consensus); MCR 83.9% favorable; guidance raised to >$18.25. DOJ Medicare Advantage criminal probe acknowledged in risk factors — no new disclosure on timeline or cooperation scope. Net +2.2 within threshold.

confirmations.name: United States
confirmations.note: DEI EO federal-contractor clause effective April 25. IBM settlement first operationalization. ICE detention at historic high 66,000+. Third-country deportation (Libya, South Sudan) flagged as potential non-refoulment violations. Structural EQU regression within threshold.

confirmations.name: Sudan
confirmations.note: Year 4 of war; 14M displaced; famine confirmed in Al Fasher and Kadugli; UN plan funded at 16% of $9B target. Zero floor held.

confirmations.name: Israel
confirmations.note: 37 INGOs deregistered (closes ~1/3 of Gaza health facilities). UN experts explicitly name EU trade suspension as 'minimum requirement' — rare naming. EU pressure building (Spain/Slovenia/Ireland) with Germany/Italy blocking. Directional within threshold.

points: Both Anthropic and OpenAI scored on existing evidence without projecting April 24 Illinois committee vote outcomes — pre-vote evidence discipline held.

Anthropic proposed 61.6 (Established) vs. OpenAI confirmed 28.8 (Developing): 33-point gap between the top-ranked frontier lab and its nearest Developing-band peer is the index's defining structural fact.
If SB 3261 advances with Anthropic support: next-cycle INT/BND positive. If SB 3444 advances with OpenAI backing (experts say unlikely): upgrade OpenAI to high-confidence downgrade proposal.
Palantir AI first baseline at 12.8 confirms the sub-20 scan expectation — ImmigrationOS and congressional oversight demand are the governing anchors.

IBM $17M DOJ FCA settlement (April 10) is the first enforcement action under the Civil Rights Fraud Initiative — operationalizes the Trump DEI EO one week before its April 25 effective date for federal contractors.

EEOC April 7 reminder letter to all Fortune 500 creates a documented notice baseline — any entity that received the letter and continues DEI-integrated incentive compensation faces the same FCA theory.
High-exposure entities not yet assessed under this framework: Boeing, Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, Raytheon, Palantir — all have federal contracts with documented DEI program histories.

Norway 84.7 Exemplary and New Zealand 78.4 Established are the pipeline's first evidence-based baselines for countries in or near the Exemplary band — both previously held unreviewed placeholder scores.
Norway's delta is ~90% attributable to the perfect-100 ceiling artifact; the genuine evidence gap (oil-welfare paradox, Sámi rights) accounts for approximately −4 to −5 points of the 15.3 total.

New Zealand's band change to Established is driven by documented equity gaps (Māori life-expectancy, Health Authority disestablishment) — not skepticism about governance quality. The Te Tiriti framework is exceptional.
Macy's (proposed 41.3) and Deere (proposed 48.1) both exhibit multi-year structural contraction: permanent facility closures, sustained layoff trajectories, no union representation.
Both Gen-2 artifacts confirmed at ranks 87-88 — uniform 3.0-3.5 profiles replaced by evidence-graded dimension scores dropping 8-12 points.
Macy's proposed 41.3 sits at the Functional/Developing boundary — one additional layoff wave or warehouse closure announcement would trigger a Developing proposal.

emergingRisks: April 24: Illinois SB 3261 / SB 3444 committee votes — material for Anthropic and OpenAI; retrieve outcome immediately; OpenAI proposal confidence upgrades to high if SB 3444 advances with OpenAI backing.

April 25: DEI EO federal-contractor clause effective — IBM settlement is proof of concept; Boeing, Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, Raytheon, Palantir are highest-exposure entities not yet assessed under FCA enforcement theory.
Before April 30: South Sudan UNMISS Security Council vote — deferred assessment must be re-queued immediately post-vote; mandate lapse would structurally collapse ACT and BND dimensions.
April 27: Musk v. OpenAI trial jury selection — daily docket monitoring; any structural ruling or settlement offer is a proposal escalation trigger.
Meta AI keylogger trajectory: EU/UK GDPR enforcement inquiry filing would provide in-window anchor for a downgrade proposal from confirmed 26.9 baseline; monitor regulatory filings in next 14 days.

F500 Gen-2 batch: Merck (published 87.7) and P&G (published 86.1) remain flagged without in-window anchors; April 25 DEI EO compliance announcements may produce event anchors for Established-band federal contractors.
Anthropic DOD litigation oral arguments May 19 — pre-argument filings to watch from May 1; potential INT/SYS dimension event depending on outcome.
insights: The Anthropic delta of −29.3 is primarily a methodology artifact, not a governance collapse. Approximately −27 points reflect a stored-composite integration-premium overrun exceeding the v1.1 H1 cap. The genuine event-driven delta is −2. External communications should separate these clearly — conflating them risks mischaracterizing Anthropic's actual evidence trajectory.

Amazon's band crossing to Critical is the day's most reputationally significant finding. A rank-2 Fortune 500 company crossing into Critical — the band reserved for entities that cause documented harm without adequate response — is an editorial claim of significant weight. The PDX9 work-around-body order and the first-ever NLRB bargaining order provide the evidentiary foundation at high confidence.

IBM's settlement is not just an IBM event. The Civil Rights Fraud Initiative theory — that DEI-integrated incentive compensation while certifying anti-discrimination compliance constitutes False Claims Act fraud — is now an established enforcement precedent. Every F500 federal contractor with DEI-tied bonuses is exposed to the same theory. The April 25 EO effective date amplifies urgency.

Norway and New Zealand as first baselines demonstrate the framework working as designed at the top of the scale: both countries receive scores that acknowledge genuine complexity rather than defaulting to reputational inference. Norway is Exemplary and its oil-welfare paradox is documented. New Zealand is Established because a 7-year Māori life-expectancy gap is documented. That is evidence-based scoring.

The F500 Generation-2 correction has now run for four consecutive nights across eleven entities without a single upward first-time baseline. Published F500 high-scorers systematically overstate performance under reputational inference. The correction phase is operating correctly; the communication strategy for it remains the open decision.
highlights: 10 change proposals tonight — record for a single pipeline run. 6 first-time baselines, all downward.
Anthropic's −29.3 delta is ~93% a calculation artifact (integration-premium overrun); genuine event-driven delta is −2. Post does not require revision.
Amazon crosses Developing → Critical: rank-2 F500 company, high confidence, worker-death and first-ever NLRB bargaining order as evidence anchors.
Norway 84.7 and New Zealand 78.4: first exemplary-band country baselines — rigor milestones for the countries index.
IBM $17M DOJ settlement operationalizes DEI FCA enforcement theory one week before April 25 federal-contractor EO effective date.

sectorAlerts.label: April 24 committee vote: SB 3261 (Anthropic-backed child safety liability) vs SB 3444 (OpenAI-backed mass-casualty liability shield). Vote outcome material for ACC/INT dimensions of both labs.

IBM $17M DOJ FCA settlement (April 10) is first Civil Rights Fraud Initiative enforcement action. DEI EO federal-contractor clause effective April 25. All F500 federal contractors with DEI-integrated compensation at risk.
Band crossing Developing → Critical. PDX9 worker death (April 6) + first-ever NLRB bargaining order (April 2). High confidence.
South Sudan UNMISS mandate expires April 30 — assessment deferred pending vote. Sudan year-4 war at 16% UN plan funding. Israel flotilla at sea.
Meta AI 'Model Capability Initiative' keylogger announced April 21, employee protests April 22. EU/UK GDPR enforcement inquiry possible. Confirmed 26.9 pending further monitoring.
